op.text: Dorothy Jean launched successfully on July 12, 2025 at Cathedral Park. Gentle afternoon sail on the Columbia River with Sam, Marcus, and four-month-old Nina aboard. Sky was 'high thin stuff, not quite a mare's tail but close' — Dana took it as a blessing. Photo exists of Nina asleep in tiny life jacket against Marcus's chest with mainsail up behind them; Dana has had it printed twice.

On July 12, 2025, the Dorothy Jean sailed for the first time since restoration. Dana, Sam, Marcus, and four-month-old Nina aboard for a gentle afternoon on the Columbia River. The sky was 'high thin stuff, not quite a mare's tail but close,' and Dana decided that counted as a blessing. There is a photo of Nina asleep in a tiny life jacket against Marcus's chest with the mainsail up behind them. Dana said: 'I have already had it printed twice.' (2025-07-19)

[as of 2025-07-19] Shipped interoperability platform in mid-July 2025 after eighteen months of development and internal politics; CEO gave public recognition in all-hands meeting. Major professional milestone.

On 2025-07-19, reflecting on the interoperability platform launch (mid-July 2025), Dana said: 'I stood there in the launch channel with all the confetti emojis and felt... nothing. Not modest-nothing. Actual, echoing nothing.' Dana described this as rattling: 'I hit the biggest professional moment of my life and it was like biting into wax fruit.' Dana said: 'For fifteen years I have basically been the person who is her work. Not in a grim way — I mean it like an engine. The next launch, the next rung, the harder problem. That's how I've always known I'm okay. It's how I know who I am. I'm the one who never turns down the bigger role. Ambition's been my whole spine.' Dana attributed the numbness to grief: 'Everything's been a half-step muted since Mom in a way that still ambushes me.' (2025-07-19)
